name: Irish Buttermilk Bannock
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large bowl, stir together the flour, baking powder, salt, and baking soda.
2. Add raisins to the dry ingredients and mix well.
3. In another bowl, fork-blend eggs and buttermilk until combined.
4. Pour the egg mixture into the dry ingredients and stir until a sticky batter is formed.
5. Place a floured surface in front of you and turn out the batter.
6. Gently knead the batter on the floured surface until it forms a smooth ball.
7. Shape the batter into a round ball and place it in a greased, non-stick casserole dish sprayed with cooking spray.
8. Make an X-shaped mark on top of the bread using a sharp knife.
9. Bake uncovered at 350°F (175°C) for about 1 1/4 hours.
10. Allow the bread to cool for 10-15 minutes before turning off and removing it from the casserole dish.
11. Transfer the bread to a wire rack to cool completely.
12. If desired, cut the loaf into quarters, then slice thinly.
